site.title: andy's builds
site.tagline: things i've made with claude code
site.name: Andy Keeley
site.role: CCO at a travel tech company

intro: I started using Claude Code to scratch my own itches — automating work stuff, tracking health data, managing investments. Somewhere along the way I realised I'd accidentally built a portfolio. Everything below was built by describing what I wanted in plain English. I've never written a line of code by hand.

projects.name: investment-agent
projects.description: Autonomous trading agent. AI-scores opportunities across stocks, crypto, and options. Executes paper trades via IBKR and Kraken Pro. Daily email digest of decisions and portfolio state.

projects.name: health-tracker
projects.description: Correlates alcohol intake with Garmin biometrics — resting HR, HRV, sleep score. Streak analysis, dose-response curves, trend charts. Guilt-as-a-service.

projects.name: customer-health-dashboard
projects.description: Reads CCO Hub exports and aged debt reports. Generates weighted health scores across the customer portfolio. Full HTML dashboard. Replaced a process that took hours.

value.tip: use it in every board pack

projects.name: ar-collections-copilot
projects.description: AI agent for chasing aged debt. Prioritises accounts, suggests chasing cadences, handles escalation paths. Goal: 60-day debtor ratio from ~40% → <10%.

projects.name: hotel-booking-app
projects.description: Full-stack booking app built on the iVectorOne Universal Hotel API. Built to demo Claude Code to our engineering team. Their reaction was the whole point.

effort.tip: my first real build, steep learning curve

value.tip: proved the concept to the team

projects.name: exercise-coach
projects.description: Multi-mode training agent with full athlete profile and training history. Four modes: LOG, REVIEW, PLAN, CHECK. Migrated from ChatGPT into Claude Code.

effort.tip: migration took a couple of sessions

projects.name: mentor-agent
projects.description: Personal Claude Code agent. GTD/EOS file structure, Google Calendar and Gmail via MCP, knowledge layer, and a Deal Prep mode for commercial negotiations. The hub everything else connects to.

value.tip: genuinely can't work without it now

projects.name: agent-launcher
projects.description: Central hub for all agents. Spawns isolated sessions via tmux. One command to get to anything.

usage.tip: every time i open a terminal

backlog.title: Client onboarding automator
backlog.description: Auto-generate implementation timelines, contract checklists, and welcome packs from CRM data

backlog.title: Competitor pricing monitor
backlog.description: Scrape and compare hotel pricing across OTAs, surface anomalies, alert on significant shifts

backlog.title: Family meal planner
backlog.description: Weekly meal plans based on dietary preferences, generates shopping lists, syncs with calendar

backlog.title: Meeting prep agent
backlog.description: Pull context from calendar, emails, and notes before each meeting. Surface talking points and outstanding actions

backlog.title: Kid's coding curriculum builder
backlog.description: Structured programming lessons that build toward game modding, auto-adapts to progress

notes: I'm a CCO, not a developer. My background is commercial — ARR growth, account management, contract negotiations. I started building because I had problems nobody else was going to solve.

Every project here was built by describing what I wanted to Claude Code in plain English, iterating on the output, and deploying. The stack listed on each card is what Claude Code chose, not what I specified.
If you've got a problem that feels like it should be software but you don't have developers to build it — [get in touch](mailto:andy@example.com). I might be able to help.
